title: A/B experiment readout
description: Data contract for the experiment-readout format: a controlled-experiment (A/B test) results readout. Header carries the hypothesis + experiment status (running / shipped / inconclusive). A hero panel reports the PRIMARY metric: control vs each variant value, the uplift % vs control, a confidence interval, and a significance verdict toned ok/risk/hold. A secondary-metrics table lists every other metric per variant with uplift, p-value and a significance flag. A per-segment breakdown lets the reader recompute the readout for one audience slice. The verdict derives from the data (p<0.05 etc.) — never authored. Renders to a centred, themeable, interactive HTML readout; the sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown above the title, e.g. 'Experiment readout'.

title.description: Experiment title, e.g. 'Checkout — single-page vs stepped flow'.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line standfirst summarising what was tested and why.

date.description: ISO date the readout was produced (YYYY-MM-DD).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. The in-report switcher can change it live.

experiment.description: The experiment design + lifecycle facts shown in the hypothesis header.

hypothesis.description: The hypothesis under test, stated as a claim, e.g. 'A single-page checkout will raise completion vs the stepped flow without harming AOV.'

owner.description: Optional owning team or experimenter name.

platform.description: Optional surface the experiment ran on, e.g. 'Web + iOS', 'Mobile app'.

end.description: ISO date the experiment ended or is scheduled to end (YYYY-MM-DD).

status.description: Lifecycle state. 'running' = still collecting; 'shipped' = winner rolled out; 'inconclusive' = stopped without a significant result; 'stopped' = halted early. Drives the header status pill tone (running→hold, shipped→ok, inconclusive/stopped→risk).

primaryMetric.description: The `key` of the metric in `metrics[]` that is the decision metric. Exactly one metric should also set primary:true and match this key. The hero panel reports this metric.

alphaPct.description: Significance threshold as a percentage; a metric is 'significant' when its pValue < alphaPct/100. Default 5 (i.e. p<0.05).

variants.description: The arms of the experiment. Exactly one MUST set isControl:true (the baseline every uplift is measured against). Render order is control first, then variants.

id.description: Stable unique key used to key `byVariant` maps in `metrics`/`segments`. e.g. 'control', 'var-single'.

isControl.description: True for the baseline arm. Exactly one variant sets this; uplift % is computed relative to it.

allocationPct.description: Optional traffic allocation as a percentage of total (the split). Allocations across variants typically sum to 100.

users.description: Optional sample size (exposed users / sessions) in this arm.

metrics.description: Every measured metric. Exactly one should set primary:true (the hero metric, matching experiment.primaryMetric); the rest populate the secondary-metrics table. Each metric carries per-variant values plus the uplift / p-value / significance computed against the control.

label.description: Metric display name, e.g. 'Checkout completion rate'.

unit.description: Optional value unit/suffix shown after each value, e.g. '%', 's', '$', '/session'.

format.description: How to render the raw value: 'percent' appends % and treats the number as already-scaled; 'currency' prefixes a currency glyph; 'duration' suffixes the unit (e.g. s); 'number' is plain. Affects display only.

betterWhen.description: Direction of improvement. 'high' = larger is better (e.g. conversion); 'low' = smaller is better (e.g. latency, refund rate). Determines whether a positive uplift reads as good or bad.

primary.description: True for the single decision metric rendered in the hero panel. At most one metric sets this and it should match experiment.primaryMetric.

byVariant.description: Per-variant result, keyed by variant id. Every variant id in `variants` should appear. Each entry holds the point value and an optional confidence interval for that value.

value.description: The metric's point estimate for this variant (in the metric's natural units, e.g. 12.8 for 12.8%).

ciLow.description: Optional lower bound of the confidence interval for this value.

ciHigh.description: Optional upper bound of the confidence interval for this value.

upliftVsControlPct.description: Optional relative change of the (non-control) variant vs control, as a percentage. With >2 variants this is the headline variant's uplift; the renderer recomputes per variant from byVariant when omitted.

pValue.description: Optional p-value of the variant-vs-control comparison. Compared against experiment.alphaPct to derive significance.

significant.description: Optional explicit significance flag. When omitted the renderer derives it from pValue < alphaPct/100.

segments.description: Optional audience slices for the per-segment breakdown. Selecting a segment recomputes the readout from that slice's numbers; the implicit first option is 'All users' (the top-level metrics).

sharePct.description: Optional share of traffic this segment represents, as a percentage.

metrics.description: Per-metric overrides for this segment, keyed by metric key. Only metrics present here are recomputed; metrics omitted fall back to the all-users numbers. Same shape as top-level metric value/uplift/p-value fields.

key.description: Metric key this override applies to (must match a metrics[].key).

byVariant.description: Per-variant value (and optional CI) for this metric within this segment, keyed by variant id.

value.description: Point estimate for this variant in this segment.

upliftVsControlPct.description: Optional precomputed uplift % for this segment; recomputed from byVariant when omitted.

pValue.description: Optional p-value for this metric within this segment.

significant.description: Optional explicit significance flag for this segment; derived from pValue when omitted.

meta.title: Checkout — single-page vs stepped flow
meta.subtitle: Six-week split test of a single-page checkout against the current three-step flow, measured on completion, revenue per session and support contacts.
meta.author: Research Desk

experiment.hypothesis: Collapsing the three-step checkout into a single page will lift checkout completion without reducing revenue per session or raising support contacts.
